paper_focus.title: Dynamic recrystallization behavior and microstructure evolution of low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel
paper_focus.research_objective: To study the dynamic recrystallization behavior and microstructure evolution of low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel during hot deformation in order to establish constitutive models and identify optimal processing windows for grain refinement.
material_systems: nominal composition (wt%): 1.46 C, 25.14 Mn, 10 Al, 0.053 Nb, balance Fe
as-forged billet with initial microstructure (austenite matrix with possible carbides)
main_process_variables: homogenization: heat to 1200 °C at 10 °C/s, hold 120 s, cool to deformation T at 5 °C/s, hold 15 s before compression
expected_information_axis: flow stress characteristics (critical, peak, steady-state stress/strain)
DRX activation energy and Zener-Hollomon parameter
microstructure evolution and DRX grain size
figure evidence (flow curves, optical/confocal micrographs, grain size correlation plots)
